notes: Initial standalone wording skill split from dg-questionnaire-designer.
Owns respondent-facing DG wording, fixed About Me wording, tone adaptation, burden reduction, and checklist-to-diary rewriting.

Promoted reviewed Claude Opus gold-data wording patterns: invitation verbs, checklist softening, returning-wave phrasing, scene reconstruction, video process invitation, conversational reason prompts, and technical hygiene checks.

0.1.2 promotes Case_005 KACO review wording rules: respondent-owned module titles, emotionally valuable intros/endings, contextual transitions, avoiding obvious yes/no and comparison prompts, light parenthetical probes, and photo-first media wording.
0.1.3 promotes product innovation wording rules: bounded future-product questions, concrete anchors, decomposed product criteria, reference-image prompts, and answerable metaphor wording.
0.1.4 makes first-person respondent-facing module titles mandatory by default and resolves the prior conflict that preserved research-style module names.
0.1.5 adds a dedicated default system prompt for the wording agent Turing-test persona experiment.

0.1.6 replaces the Turing-test persona with a task-editor system prompt and promotes gold-data intro/ending rules: task-entry intros, light task transitions, diary completion endings, and bans on AI-host style over-thanking.
0.1.7 adds minimal-edit wording rules after Case_005 comparison: preserve already-natural designer wording, avoid tone flattening, and only rewrite clear wording problems.
0.1.8 adds the single-point question rule and multi_intent_question rubric label: preserve same-point follow-ups, but split or narrow questions that combine different information goals.
